items.title: Endpoint returns the funnel contract for an authorized org
items.precondition: A valid JWT for a user in a pilot org with at least one location (see reference_auth_testing). Backend running.
steps: 1. GET /api/patient-journey with the Authorization: Bearer <token> header.
2. (optional) add ?month=2026-06 to pin the report month.
3. Inspect the JSON body.
items.expected: 200 with { success: true, data: { location, period, stages[5], conversions[4], leakStageKey, revenue, context:{rank,reviews}, headline } }. stages keys are market_demand, impressions, visits, leads, patients in that order.

items.title: Endpoint requires authentication
steps: 1. GET /api/patient-journey with NO Authorization header.
items.expected: 401 with the { success:false, error } envelope — the default-deny auth guard blocks the request before any data read.

items.title: Cross-tenant location request is denied
items.precondition: A valid JWT for org A; a locationId that belongs to org B.
steps: 1. GET /api/patient-journey?locationId=<org-B-location-id> with org A's token.
items.expected: 403 LOCATION_ACCESS_DENIED (no rows from org B leak); the requested locationId must match the middleware-authorized location.

items.title: Patient Journey screen renders the six-stage pipeline
items.precondition: Logged in as a pilot org user (LocationContext resolved to a real location).
steps: 1. Navigate to /patientJourneyInsights (or click the Patient Journey nav item).
2. Observe the horizontal card + arrow pipeline.
items.expected: Five funnel stages render as cards joined by arrows (Searching your market → Saw your listing → Visited your site → Reached out → Became patients), plus rank/reviews context and a one-thing headline. Live numbers where sources are connected.

items.title: Honest empty states for unconnected sources
items.precondition: A pilot org with at least one missing source (e.g. no PMS upload, or search volume not harvested yet).
steps: 1. Open /patientJourneyInsights for that org.
2. Look at the stage whose source is missing (e.g. Became patients with no PMS, or Searching your market with no search volume).
items.expected: That stage shows a 'not connected yet' empty state — never a misleading zero. The rest of the funnel still renders (graceful per-stage degradation).

items.title: Biggest-leak emphasis and per-step conversion %
items.precondition: A pilot org with at least two connected adjacent stages.
steps: 1. Open /patientJourneyInsights.
2. Read the conversion % on each arrow and the highlighted leak step + headline.
items.expected: Per-step conversion %s shown; the single smallest non-null step is visually emphasized as the biggest leak, and the descriptive headline (not a prediction) names that step.

items.title: Visits is labelled all-channel, not a strict click-through
items.precondition: A pilot org with Rybbit connected.
steps: 1. Open /patientJourneyInsights.
2. Read the 'Visited your site' stage label/tooltip and the Saw → Visited step.
items.expected: 'Visited your site' uses Rybbit visits, labelled all-channel (search + direct + social, best-effort bot-filtered); the Saw → Visited step is presented as a visit count, not a strict search CTR.

items.title: Per-stage freshness ('as of') is shown
items.precondition: A pilot org with connected sources of differing lag.
steps: 1. Open /patientJourneyInsights.
2. Look for the per-stage 'as of' date on each stage.
items.expected: Each connected stage shows its own freshness date; there is no single misleading global 'as of'.

items.title: Org-type wording resolves (Patient vs Customer)
items.precondition: Access to a health-type org and a generic-type org.
steps: 1. Open /patientJourneyInsights as a health-type org — read the title and the final stage.
2. Open it as a generic-type org — read the same.
items.expected: Health org shows 'Patient Journey Insights' and 'Became patients'; generic org shows 'Customer Journey Insights' and 'Became customers'. No hardcoded clinical word.

items.title: Multi-location practice labels shared traffic stages
steps: 1. Open /patientJourneyInsights for the multi-location org.
2. Read the shared website-traffic stages (Saw / Visited / Reached out).
items.expected: The shared website-traffic stages carry a 'whole-practice website' label; the per-location stages (search volume / rank / reviews / patients) stay location-specific.

items.title: Navigation item appears and routes correctly
items.precondition: Logged in pilot org user, desktop and mobile widths.
steps: 1. On desktop, find the Patient Journey item in the sidebar; click it.
2. Resize to mobile; confirm it appears in the bottom nav and routes.
items.expected: Nav item is visible on desktop sidebar and mobile bottom nav, routes to /patientJourneyInsights, and shows the active state when on that screen.

items.title: Practice Hub summary card renders and links through
steps: 1. Open the Practice Hub (/dashboard).
2. Find the Patient Journey summary card (after the One Thing banner).
3. Click its link.
items.expected: A compact Patient Journey card renders with a headline/biggest-leak one-liner (honest state when data is sparse) and links to /patientJourneyInsights.

items.title: Reduced-motion is respected
steps: 1. Enable prefers-reduced-motion.
2. Open /patientJourneyInsights.
items.expected: Entrance / count-up / hover animations are suppressed or minimized; the screen is fully usable with no motion.
